title: ARG is not expanded in COPY
body:
I have following dockerfile (simplified to bare minimum):
```
  FROM ubuntu:18.04
  ARG TARGET=debug
  COPY target/${TARGET}/mayastor /usr/local/bin/
  ENTRYPOINT ["/usr/local/bin/mayastor"]
```
The ARG TARGET is not expanded in COPY action. So instead of `target/debug/mayastor` the COPY action is trying to copy `target/mayastor` file. It works when building the image using docker. Is it unsupported feature? Or am I doing anything wrong?

I'm using:
```
buildah version 1.8-dev (image-spec 1.0.0, runtime-spec 1.0.0)
```
on Ubuntu 18.04
```
Linux mayastor-dev 4.15.0-43-generic #46-Ubuntu SMP Thu Dec 6 14:45:28 UTC 2018 x86_64 x86_64 x86_64 GNU/Linux
```

comments.body:
@jkryl The Dockerfile syntax that you're using is valid from what I can see.  I tried in on Fedora 29 using the latest Buildah from upstream and I saw:
```
STEP 3: COPY target/${TARGET}/mayastor /usr/local/bin/
error building at STEP "COPY target/${TARGET}/mayastor /usr/local/bin/": no files found matching "/root/target/debug/mayastor": no such file or directory
```
Which although an error as I don't have the directory, it shows the expand TARGET variable.

Couple things:

Can you try running the command again with --debug? I did `buildah --debug bud -t myimg -f ~/Dockerfile .`

You shouldn't need to do this, but try passing TARGET as a build arg: `buildah bud -t myimg -f ~/Dockerfile --build-arg TARGET=debug .`

Do you by chance have a Fedora 29 VM that you can try this on?  I'm not sure why there might be a difference between the two, maybe some kind of shell funkiness?

@TomSweeneyRedHat : thanks for your reply. Most likely you are right, because when I build the same image in fedora container, it works fine (though on fedora I have slightly older buildah version). So it seems that buildah on ubuntu is not expanding vars. This is the output with debug flag:

the above is with build-arg option. Without build-arg option it is the same.
@TomSweeneyRedHat Thanks much for the confirmation, it will let us focus on Ubuntu then.

Sorry, I have to correct myself. I created a new image based on fedora with buildah:
```
FROM fedora:latest
RUN dnf install -y \
  buildah \
  podman \
  make \
  && \
 dnf clean all
```
buildah and podman versions:
```
$ podman version
Version:            1.2.0
RemoteAPI Version:  1
Go Version:         go1.11.5
OS/Arch:            linux/amd64
$ buildah version
Version:         1.7
Go Version:      go1.11.5
Image Spec:      1.0.0
Runtime Spec:    1.0.0
CNI Spec:        0.4.0
libcni Version:  
Git Commit:      
Built:           Thu Jan  1 00:00:00 1970
OS/Arch:         linux/amd64
```
and it fails to expand variables too. That is quite mysterious. I hope to have more time next week to gather some more info about the case.
I have tested buildah again with the new version 1.8 and it works fine. Thanks! I'm closing the ticket.
